name: Rice, Parmesan and Dried Tomato Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Chicken broth or vegetable broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine onion and olive oil in a large, wide skillet with a tight-fitting lid.
2. Cook, stirring, over low heat until the onion is tender, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in rice, dried tomatoes, and rosemary. Cook and stir for 1 minute over low heat.
4. Add broth and salt. Bring to boil over high heat, stirring once. Cover and cook over low heat until liquid is absorbed, about 15 minutes. Uncover and stir in cheese. Let stand until lukewarm. This step can be done ahead of time and refrigerated for several days.
5. Add egg and stir to blend the mixture.
6. Using a 1/4 cup measure, form rice mixture into small pancakes about 2 inches in diameter and 1/2 inch thick. Rinse hands frequently with cold water to prevent sticking.
7. Coat a large, non-stick skillet or griddle with a thin film of olive oil. Heat over medium heat.
8. Add pancakes in batches and cook until golden brown on one side, about 5 minutes. Carefully turn the pancakes using a wide spatula, and brown the other side. Keep warm in an oven set to its lowest temperature until all are done.
Recommended side dishes: Escarole with Garlic, Roasted Peppers
